medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ presented to the ED for evaluation of left hand numbness and weakness. He awoke to find that he had bitten his tongue and had wet himself, possibly due to a seizure. He experienced sudden onset of vertigo, hit his head against the wall, and later noted left hand weakness and numbness. He had intermittent confusion. His last known seizure was in ___ and he has not had any signs of infection or missed medication doses.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypertension, Dyslipidemia, COPD, ?Seizures, Bipolar disorder, Legally blind s/p multiple surgeries.
medical_history.family_history: Father deceased at age ___ from MI. Mother in late ___. 3 brothers and 2 sisters who are healthy. No known family history of seizures or strokes.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: T: 97.5 P: 89 R: 18 BP: 135/75 SaO2: 94%. General: Awake, cooperative, NAD. No remarkable findings noted.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurologic: Alert, oriented x 3, able to follow commands. Motor: Normal bulk, tone. Diminished light touch over dorsum of left hand, intact sensation elsewhere. DTRs: Plantar response was flexor bilaterally. Gait: stable ambulation observed.
